ibis.orig: Whether or not long covid counts in law as a disability
ibis.text: Does long covid count in law as a disability?

ibis.orig: In cases where long covid has a substantial effect on someone’s ability to carry out day-to-day activities and where it lasts or seems likely to last at least 12 months, it will be considered a disability in law.
ibis.text: Long covid is considered a disability in law if it has a substantial effect on day-to-day activities and lasts or is likely to last at least 12 months.

ibis.orig: the legal definition of a disability, given in section 6 of the Equality Act 2010, is an impairment that has a “substantial” and “long-term” adverse effect on someone’s “ability to carry out normal day-to-day activities”.
ibis.text: The legal definition of a disability under section 6 of the Equality Act 2010 is an impairment with a substantial and long-term adverse effect on a person's ability to carry out normal day-to-day activities.

ibis.orig: Employers should focus on the reasonable adjustments they can make rather than trying to work out if an employee's condition is a disability.
ibis.text: Employers should focus on the reasonable adjustments they can make rather than trying to determine if an employee's condition constitutes a disability.

ibis.orig: what extra flexibility can be offered to workers with long covid
ibis.text: What extra flexibility can be offered to workers with long covid?

ibis.orig: plans to expand the right to request flexible working, some of which were being delivered by the Employment Relations (Flexible Working) Act 2023
ibis.text: The Government is expanding the right to request flexible working through the Employment Relations (Flexible Working) Act 2023.

ibis.orig: what financial support is available to people unable to work due to long covid, particularly former key workers
ibis.text: What financial support is available to people unable to work due to long covid, particularly former key workers?

ibis.orig: the Government had no plans to introduce additional special support for key workers with long covid
ibis.text: The Government does not plan to introduce additional special support specifically for key workers with long covid.

ibis.orig: The DWP already offers financial support through Universal Credit and New Style Employment and Support Allowance for people who are unable to work due to long-Covid, subject to them satisfying the eligibility criteria and contribution conditions. In addition, people with Long-Covid may be eligible for Personal Independence Payment. This support is available regardless of keyworker status.
ibis.text: Financial support via Universal Credit, New Style Employment and Support Allowance, and Personal Independence Payment is already available to those unable to work due to long covid regardless of their status as a key worker.

ibis.orig: Long covid is not currently prescribed as an occupational disease for the purposes of Industrial Injuries Disablement Benefit
ibis.text: Should long covid be prescribed as an occupational disease for the purposes of Industrial Injuries Disablement Benefit?

ibis.orig: IIAC recommended the following be added to the list of prescribed diseases allowing entitlement to IIDB
ibis.text: The Industrial Injuries Advisory Council recommends that specific long covid complications be added to the list of prescribed diseases for IIDB entitlement.

ibis.orig: IIAC found "convincing and consistent evidence" of five serious pathological complications for health and social workers with long covid. These complications had been shown to cause persistent impairment and loss of function in some workers.
ibis.text: The IIAC found convincing and consistent evidence that five serious pathological complications of long covid cause persistent impairment and loss of function in health and social workers.
